items.title: SonicWall warns of SMA1000 flaws exploited in zero-day attacks, patch now
items.description: SonicWall warns that threat actors have been exploiting two SMA1000 vulnerabilities, tracked asCVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, in zero-day attacks and urges customers to install the newly released security updates. [...]

id: 7a3bd18c-ed9b-5267-a629-6800946d6cc9
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-15410 (Web Application).

    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: KEV_EXPLOIT
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-15410
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/15
modified: 2026/07/15
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-15410 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-15T01:40:04Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-15410", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-15410 Exploitation Attempt - KEV_EXPLOIT"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:5143460; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-15, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Trivy: Helm chart tar bomb causes OOM via unbounded io.ReadAll in parser
items.description:
## Summary

When Trivy scans a Helm chart archive (`.tgz`), its custom tar unpacker reads each entry with `io.ReadAll(tr)` and no size limit. An attacker who can place a malicious `.tgz` file in the scanned path can craft a small compressed archive that decompresses to gigabytes, causing the Trivy process to be killed by the OS OOM killer.

## Affected configurations

Exploitation requires the attacker to place a crafted `.tgz` file in a location that Trivy will scan as a Helm chart. This applies to the following scan targets:

| Command | Condition |
| --- | --- |
| `trivy config ` | Directory contains a crafted `.tgz` Helm chart (misconfiguration scanning is always enabled) |
| `trivy filesystem --scanners misconf ` | Directory contains a crafted `.tgz` Helm chart **and** `--sc

items.title: Woodpecker gRPC agent_id metadata can be spoofed- cross-tenant agent impersonation
items.description:
### Impact
A vulnerability in Woodpecker CI's gRPC layer allowed any authenticated agent to impersonate any other agent on the same server by injecting a forged `agent_id` value into outgoing gRPC metadata. The server correctly verified the JWT token but then discarded the verified agent identity in favor of the client-supplied value.

### Patches
Direct patch: https://github.com/woodpecker-ci/woodpecker/pull/6567
Later proper fix: https://github.com/woodpecker-ci/woodpecker/pull/6569

### Workarounds
Disable org agents (`WOODPECKER_DISABLE_USER_AGENT_REGISTRATION=true`) and delete existing ones

### Resources
Public ref: https://github.com/woodpecker-ci/woodpecker/issues/6541
Private com: https://github.com/woodpecker-ci/woodpecker-security/issues/21

items.title: CVE-2026-57382: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in Mitchell Bennis Si
items.description: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in Mitchell Bennis Simple File List simple-file-list allows Reflected XSS.This issue affects Simple File List: from n/a through <= 6.3.8.
items.apex_ai_summary: [HIGH] CVE advisory. Risk score 7.1/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items.ioc_note: Actor-attribution campaign — IOC set derived from threat actor research, not automated feed extraction.

apex.ai_summary: [P2] Vulnerability - high severity (Risk 7.1/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. 2 indicators detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. AI confidence: LOW (22%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: HIGH PRIORITY - Patch within 72 hours. Monitor affected assets. Review threat hunting queries for related TTPs.

ioc_paywall.message: 2 IOC(s) at 16.0% confidence - unlock with Pro tier.

items.nvd_disclosure: This CVE ID was not found in the NIST NVD database at time of enrichment. Intelligence is preliminary and unverified by NVD. CVSS score and severity are analyst-estimated until NVD confirmation.
items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-57382 Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation Cross
id: 329f0b79-f29b-52c6-b8a5-897c7c6c8c98
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-57382 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: XSS
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-57382
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/15
modified: 2026/07/15
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: webserver
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-57382 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-15T01:40:04Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-57382", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-57382 Exploitation Attempt - XSS"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:5858793; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-15, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Medium confidence — Source 'nvd_cve' is authoritative (government/standards body)
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window
